title: 🦞 Discord - OpenClaw Docs
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# Discord. Ready for DMs and guild channels via the official Discord gateway. Discord DMs default to pairing mode. You will need to create a new application with a bot, add the bot to your server, and pair it to OpenClaw. - ### Create a Discord application and bot. Set the **Username** to whatever you call your OpenClaw agent. This is your **Bot Token** and you will need it shortly. - ### Generate an invite URL and add the bot to your server. You'll generate an invite URL with the right permissions to add the bot to your server. This is the baseline set for normal text channels. If you plan to post in Discord threads, including forum or media channel workflows that create or continue a thread, also enable **Send Messages in Threads**. You should now see your bot in the Discord server. For pairing to work, Discord needs to allow your bot to DM you.

title: 🦞 OpenClaw x Discord Server Integration Guide | Meta Intelligence
content:

# OpenClaw x Discord Server Integration Guide. Architecture diagram of OpenClaw integrated with Discord server. After embedding an OpenClaw AI agent into a Discord server, your community gains the following capabilities:. Compared to Telegram's personal remote control scenario or Slack's enterprise workflow scenario, the Discord x OpenClaw integration is best suited for **community management, open-source project support, and large-scale multi-user interaction**. Step 1: Create a Discord Bot and Obtain Token. This is the foundation of the entire **OpenClaw Discord setup** process -- creating an Application and Bot on the Discord Developer Portal and obtaining the necessary authentication Token. ### 3.1 Create a Discord Application. ### 3.2 Create Bot and Obtain Token. **Important security reminder:** The Bot Token is equivalent to full access credentials for the Bot -- anyone who has it can control your Bot. Never expose the Token in public places (GitHub, Discord channels, screenshots). After obtaining the Discord Bot Token, the next step is to write it into OpenClaw's configuration so the Gateway can connect to Discord as your Bot.

content:

# Best Chat App for OpenClaw: Telegram, Discord, or Slack? Best chat app for OpenClaw compared: Telegram wins for most users, Discord for power users, Slack for teams. Here is how to pick the right one. ## Which OpenClaw channel is right for you? OpenClaw (previously known as Clawdbot and Moltbot) connects to Telegram, Discord, Slack, Signal, WhatsApp, and several other platforms through a single gateway process. | Team already on Slack | Slack | Don't move your team for this |. For running multiple channels at once (say, Telegram for personal use and Discord for a team), see OpenClaw multi-agent and multi-channel setup guide. Telegram is the fastest, most stable path to a working OpenClaw setup. It uses the official Telegram Bot API via grammY, runs on long-polling by default, and doesn't need a public IP address or a webhook server. Full setup: OpenClaw Telegram Setup Guide. Full setup: OpenClaw Discord Setup Guide.

title: 🦞 Telegram - OpenClaw Docs
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

Telegram bots default to **Privacy Mode**, which limits what group messages they receive. Practical pattern for one-owner bots: set your user ID in `channels.telegram.allowFrom`, leave `groupAllowFrom` unset, and allow the target groups under `channels.telegram.groups`. {channels: {telegram: { enabled: true, dmPolicy: "pairing", allowFrom: ["<YOUR_TELEGRAM_USER_ID>"], groupPolicy: "allowlist", groups: { "<GROUP_CHAT_ID>": { requireMention: true, }, },},},}. DM topic routing now follows the bot capability from Telegram `getMe.has_topics_enabled`, which is controlled by BotFather threaded mode: topics-enabled bots use thread-scoped DM sessions when Telegram sends `message_thread_id`; other DMs stay on the flat session. DM chats with `message_thread_id` keep reply metadata; they use thread-aware session keys only when Telegram `getMe` reports `has_topics_enabled: true` for the bot. * `channels.telegram.mediaGroupFlushMs` (default 500) controls how long Telegram albums/media groups are buffered before OpenClaw dispatches them as one inbound message. * group context history uses `channels.telegram.historyLimit` or `messages.groupChat.historyLimit` (default 50); `0` disables. title: 🦞 Scheduled tasks - OpenClaw Docs
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

Cron is the Gateway's built-in scheduler. It persists jobs, wakes the agent at the right time, and can deliver output back to a chat channel or webhook endpoint. * On upgrade, run `openclaw doctor --fix` to import legacy `~/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json`, `jobs-state.json`, and `runs/*.jsonl` files into SQLite and rename them with a `.migrated` suffix. After import, editing that JSON file no longer changes active cron jobs; use `openclaw cron add|edit|remove` or the Gateway cron RPC methods instead. * Isolated cron runs that receive the narrow cron self-cleanup grant can still read scheduler status, a self-filtered list of their current job, and that job's run history, so status/heartbeat checks can inspect their own schedule without gaining broader cron mutation access. * Isolated cron runs also treat run-level agent failures as job errors even when no reply payload is produced, so model/provider failures increment error counters and trigger failure notifications instead of clearing the job as successful.

title: 🦞 Cron - OpenClaw
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# Cron. # `openclaw cron`. Manage cron jobs for the Gateway scheduler. `openclaw cron create` is an alias for `openclaw cron add`. For new jobs, put the schedule first and the prompt second:. Use `--webhook`  when the job should POST the finished payload instead of delivering to a chat target:. Use `--command` for deterministic shell-style jobs that should run inside OpenClaw cron without starting an isolated agent/model run:. Command jobs capture stdout/stderr, record normal cron history, and route output through the same `announce`, `webhook`, or `none` delivery modes as isolated jobs. `openclaw cron list` and `openclaw cron show`  preview the resolved delivery route. Isolated cron chat delivery is shared between the agent and the runner:. Use `cron add|create --webhook`  or `cron edit  --webhook`  to set webhook delivery. For isolated jobs that target a local configured model provider, cron runs a lightweight provider preflight before starting the agent turn.

title: 🦞 OpenClaw Cron Scheduled Tasks Guide | MI
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# OpenClaw Cron Scheduled Tasks Guide. Diagram of OpenClaw Cron scheduled task automation execution. What Is OpenClaw's Cron? If you're familiar with Linux's `crontab`, OpenClaw's Cron is conceptually similar -- it automatically executes tasks at specified times. But the key difference is: traditional crontab executes fixed Shell commands, while OpenClaw's Cron executes **natural language instructions**, with the AI agent autonomously deciding how to complete them. `"Every day at 9 AM, check server status and send a summary to Telegram"`. The latter doesn't require you to write scripts in advance -- the agent will decide which check commands to execute, how to organize the results, and how to format the report on its own. `openclaw cron add --schedule "0 9 * * *" --message "Check the server's CPU and memory usage. If a Cron task's execution time exceeds this value, the Gateway will forcibly terminate the connection, causing the task to be truncated -- the agent may have completed 80% of the work, but cannot return results due to the timeout.
